description: Advanced PostgreSQL Monitoring Dashboard for SAMPLEF — covers Application-level and DB-level metrics including connections, slow queries, lock waits, cache hit ratio, table/index health, vacuum, replication, wait events, and schema inventory. Datasource: grafana-postgresql-datasource (samplef_gf_ent_dev @ Azure PostgreSQL).

panels.description: Number of backend connections currently executing a query (state = active).

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state = 'active' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid()

panels.title: Active Connections

panels.description: Connections open but not executing any query.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state = 'idle' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid()

panels.title: Idle Connections

panels.description: Connections sitting idle inside an open transaction — extremely dangerous for lock escalation and table bloat.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state ILIKE '%idle in transaction%' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid()

panels.title: Idle in Transaction

panels.description: PostgreSQL configured max_connections limit.

targets.rawSql: SELECT setting::int AS value FROM pg_settings WHERE name = 'max_connections'

panels.title: Max Connections

panels.description: Percentage of max_connections currently used. >80% is danger zone.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / (SELECT setting::int FROM pg_settings WHERE name='max_connections'), 2) AS value FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE pid <> pg_backend_pid()

panels.title: Connection Utilisation %

panels.description: Running PostgreSQL server version.

targets.rawSql: SELECT SPLIT_PART(version(), ' ', 2) AS value

panels.title: PostgreSQL Version

panels.description: Donut chart of all backend connection states — active, idle, idle in transaction, etc.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(state, 'unknown') AS state, COUNT(*) AS connections FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE pid <> pg_backend_pid() GROUP BY state ORDER BY connections DESC

panels.title: Connection State Breakdown

panels.description: Which applications, databases and users are consuming connections right now.

targets.rawSql: SELECT datname AS "Database", usename AS "User", application_name AS "Application", client_addr::text AS "Client IP", state AS "State", COUNT(*) AS "Connections" FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE pid <> pg_backend_pid() GROUP BY datname, usename, application_name, client_addr, state ORDER BY "Connections" DESC LIMIT 50

panels.title: Connections by App / DB / User

panels.description: All non-idle queries currently running. Threshold lowered to 1s so you always see activity. Empty result = healthy idle DB. Color coding: yellow > 5s, red > 30s.

targets.rawSql: SELECT pid AS "PID", usename AS "User", datname AS "Database", application_name AS "App", client_addr::text AS "Client", state AS "State", ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - query_start))::numeric, 2) AS "Duration (s)", COALESCE(wait_event_type, 'None') AS "Wait Type", COALESCE(wait_event, 'None') AS "Wait Event", LEFT(query, 300) AS "Query" FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state != 'idle' AND query_start IS NOT NULL AND NOW() - query_start > INTERVAL '1 second' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid() ORDER BY "Duration (s)" DESC LIMIT 50

panels.title: Active Queries Running > 1s (Long-Running Monitor)

panels.description: Number of queries currently blocked waiting for locks.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE cardinality(pg_blocking_pids(pid)) > 0

panels.title: Queries Blocked by Locks

panels.description: Transactions that have been open longest — these hold locks and bloat tables.

targets.rawSql: SELECT pid AS "PID", usename AS "User", datname AS "Database", application_name AS "App", state AS "State", ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - xact_start))::numeric, 2) AS "Open (s)", LEFT(query, 150) AS "Last Query" FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE xact_start IS NOT NULL AND pid <> pg_backend_pid() ORDER BY xact_start ASC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Oldest Open Transactions

panels.description: Shows which query is blocking which. Critical for diagnosing lock chains.

targets.rawSql: SELECT blocked.pid AS "Blocked PID", blocked.usename AS "Blocked User", blocked.datname AS "Database", ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM NOW() - blocked.query_start)::numeric,2) AS "Wait (s)", LEFT(blocked.query, 200) AS "Blocked Query", blocking.pid AS "Blocking PID", blocking.usename AS "Blocking User", LEFT(blocking.query, 200) AS "Blocking Query" FROM pg_stat_activity blocked JOIN pg_stat_activity blocking ON blocking.pid = ANY(pg_blocking_pids(blocked.pid)) WHERE cardinality(pg_blocking_pids(blocked.pid)) > 0 ORDER BY "Wait (s)" DESC

panels.title: Lock Waits — Who Is Blocking Whom

panels.description: All current granted and waiting locks grouped by mode and type.

targets.rawSql: SELECT mode AS "Lock Mode", locktype AS "Lock Type", COUNT(*) AS "Count", SUM(CASE WHEN granted THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Granted", SUM(CASE WHEN NOT granted THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Waiting" FROM pg_locks GROUP BY mode, locktype ORDER BY "Count" DESC

panels.title: Current Lock Grants by Mode & Type

panels.description: Tables most frequently involved in lock waits — identifies hot-spots.

targets.rawSql: SELECT n.nspname AS "Schema", c.relname AS "Table", l.mode AS "Mode", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE l.granted) AS "Granted", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT l.granted) AS "Waiting" FROM pg_locks l JOIN pg_class c ON c.oid = l.relation JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace WHERE c.relkind = 'r' GROUP BY n.nspname, c.relname, l.mode ORDER BY "Waiting" DESC, "Granted" DESC LIMIT 30

panels.title: Table-Level Lock Contention

panels.description: % of data reads served from shared_buffers (memory). Should be > 99% on a healthy DB.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(blks_hit) / NULLIF(SUM(blks_hit + blks_read), 0), 2) AS value FROM pg_stat_database

panels.title: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio (All DBs)

panels.description: % of index scans served from cache.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(idx_blks_hit) / NULLIF(SUM(idx_blks_hit + idx_blks_read), 0), 2) AS value FROM pg_statio_all_tables

panels.title: Index Hit Ratio

panels.description: % of table data read from cache (heap blocks only).

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(heap_blks_hit) / NULLIF(SUM(heap_blks_hit + heap_blks_read), 0), 2) AS value FROM pg_statio_all_tables

panels.title: Heap Hit Ratio

panels.description: Per-database breakdown: cache hit %, commits, rollbacks, rows inserted/updated/deleted.

targets.rawSql: SELECT datname AS "Database", ROUND(100.0 * blks_hit / NULLIF(blks_hit + blks_read, 0), 2) AS "Cache Hit %", xact_commit AS "Txn Commits", xact_rollback AS "Txn Rollbacks", ROUND(100.0 * xact_rollback / NULLIF(xact_commit + xact_rollback, 0), 2) AS "Rollback %", tup_inserted AS "Rows Inserted", tup_updated AS "Rows Updated", tup_deleted AS "Rows Deleted", tup_returned AS "Rows Returned" FROM pg_stat_database WHERE datname NOT IN ('template0','template1') ORDER BY "Txn Commits" DESC

panels.title: Per-Database Stats
🔍 Top Queries & Statement Statistics (pg_stat_statements — extension must be enabled)

panels.description: Top 20 most expensive queries by cumulative execution time. Requires pg_stat_statements extension. If this shows 'No data', run: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_stat_statements; and set shared_preload_libraries = 'pg_stat_statements' in postgresql.conf.

targets.rawSql: SELECT LEFT(query, 150) AS "Query", calls AS "Calls", ROUND(total_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Total Time (ms)", ROUND(mean_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Avg Time (ms)", ROUND(min_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Min (ms)", ROUND(max_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Max (ms)", ROUND(stddev_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Stddev (ms)", rows AS "Rows", ROUND(100.0 * shared_blks_hit / NULLIF(shared_blks_hit + shared_blks_read, 0), 2) AS "Cache Hit %" FROM pg_stat_statements WHERE query NOT ILIKE '%pg_stat_statements%' AND query NOT ILIKE 'BEGIN%' AND query NOT ILIKE 'COMMIT%' ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Top 20 Queries — Total Execution Time

panels.description: Top 20 slowest queries by average execution time with >= 3 calls. Requires pg_stat_statements.

targets.rawSql: SELECT LEFT(query, 150) AS "Query", calls AS "Calls", ROUND(mean_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Avg Time (ms)", ROUND(min_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Min (ms)", ROUND(max_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Max (ms)", ROUND(stddev_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Stddev (ms)", rows AS "Rows" FROM pg_stat_statements WHERE calls >= 3 AND query NOT ILIKE '%pg_stat_statements%' ORDER BY mean_exec_time DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Top 20 Slowest Queries — Avg Execution Time

panels.description: Top 20 most-called queries. High call count even for fast queries can indicate N+1 problems or missing caching.

targets.rawSql: SELECT LEFT(query, 150) AS "Query", calls AS "Calls", ROUND(total_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Total Time (ms)", ROUND(mean_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS "Avg Time (ms)", rows AS "Rows", ROUND(100.0 * shared_blks_hit / NULLIF(shared_blks_hit + shared_blks_read, 0), 2) AS "Cache Hit %" FROM pg_stat_statements WHERE query NOT ILIKE '%pg_stat_statements%' ORDER BY calls DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Top 20 Most-Called Queries

panels.description: High seq_scan on large tables usually indicates a missing index.

targets.rawSql: SELECT schemaname AS "Schema", relname AS "Table", seq_scan AS "Seq Scans", seq_tup_read AS "Seq Rows Read", idx_scan AS "Index Scans", n_live_tup AS "Live Rows", n_dead_tup AS "Dead Rows", ROUND(100.0 * n_dead_tup / NULLIF(n_live_tup + n_dead_tup, 0), 2) AS "Bloat %", last_vacuum::text AS "Last Vacuum", last_autovacuum::text AS "Last Autovacuum" FROM pg_stat_user_tables ORDER BY seq_scan DESC LIMIT 30

panels.title: Table Stats — Seq Scans vs Index Scans + Bloat

panels.description: Unused or rarely-used indexes — waste disk space and slow down write operations.

targets.rawSql: SELECT t.schemaname AS "Schema", t.relname AS "Table", i.indexrelname AS "Index", i.idx_scan AS "Idx Scans", i.idx_tup_read AS "Tuples Read", i.idx_tup_fetch AS "Tuples Fetched", pg_size_pretty(pg_relation_size(i.indexrelid)) AS "Index Size" FROM pg_stat_user_indexes i JOIN pg_stat_user_tables t ON i.relid = t.relid ORDER BY i.idx_scan ASC, pg_relation_size(i.indexrelid) DESC LIMIT 30

panels.title: Index Usage — Unused / Low-Use Indexes

panels.description: Top tables by total disk footprint (table data + indexes + TOAST).

targets.rawSql: SELECT n.nspname AS "Schema", c.relname AS "Table", pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size(c.oid)) AS "Total Size", pg_total_relation_size(c.oid) AS "Total Size (bytes)", pg_size_pretty(pg_relation_size(c.oid)) AS "Table Size", pg_size_pretty(pg_indexes_size(c.oid)) AS "Indexes Size", COALESCE(s.n_live_tup, 0) AS "Live Rows", COALESCE(s.n_dead_tup, 0) AS "Dead Rows", ROUND(COALESCE(100.0 * s.n_dead_tup / NULLIF(s.n_live_tup + s.n_dead_tup, 0), 0), 2) AS "Bloat %" FROM pg_class c JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace LEFT JOIN pg_stat_user_tables s ON s.relid = c.oid WHERE c.relkind = 'r' AND n.nspname NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema','pg_toast') ORDER BY pg_total_relation_size(c.oid) DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Largest Tables by Size

panels.description: Indexes on the same columns — candidates for consolidation or removal.

targets.rawSql: SELECT t.relname AS "Table", array_agg(i.relname ORDER BY i.relname) AS "Duplicate Indexes", ix.indkey::text AS "Column Keys", pg_size_pretty(SUM(pg_relation_size(i.oid))) AS "Wasted Size" FROM pg_index ix JOIN pg_class t ON t.oid = ix.indrelid JOIN pg_class i ON i.oid = ix.indexrelid JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = t.relnamespace WHERE n.nspname NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema','pg_toast') GROUP BY t.relname, ix.indkey HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY t.relname

panels.title: Duplicate / Redundant Indexes

panels.description: Tables with dead tuple ratio > 10% that urgently need vacuuming.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM pg_stat_user_tables WHERE n_dead_tup > 0 AND (n_dead_tup::float / NULLIF(n_live_tup + n_dead_tup, 0)) > 0.10

panels.title: Tables Needing Vacuum

panels.description: Currently active autovacuum worker processes.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE query ILIKE 'autovacuum:%'

panels.title: Active Autovacuum Workers

panels.description: Tables with highest bloat — dead tuple percentage > threshold.

targets.rawSql: SELECT schemaname AS "Schema", relname AS "Table", n_live_tup AS "Live Rows", n_dead_tup AS "Dead Rows", ROUND(100.0 * n_dead_tup / NULLIF(n_live_tup + n_dead_tup, 0), 2) AS "Bloat %", last_vacuum::text AS "Last Vacuum", last_autovacuum::text AS "Last Autovacuum", last_analyze::text AS "Last Analyze", last_autoanalyze::text AS "Last Auto Analyze" FROM pg_stat_user_tables ORDER BY "Bloat %" DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Tables with High Dead Tuple Bloat

panels.description: Live view of vacuum/analyze operations currently in progress.

targets.rawSql: SELECT pid AS "PID", datname AS "Database", relid::regclass AS "Table", phase AS "Phase", heap_blks_scanned AS "Heap Scanned", heap_blks_total AS "Heap Total", CASE WHEN heap_blks_total > 0 THEN ROUND(100.0 * heap_blks_scanned / heap_blks_total, 1) ELSE 0 END AS "% Done" FROM pg_stat_progress_vacuum

panels.title: Currently Running Vacuum / Analyze

panels.description: Snapshot of what active backends are currently waiting for.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(wait_event_type, 'CPU / No Wait') AS "Wait Type", COUNT(*) AS "Count" FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state != 'idle' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid() GROUP BY wait_event_type ORDER BY "Count" DESC

panels.title: Wait Event Type Distribution

panels.description: Detailed breakdown: wait event name + type for all non-idle sessions.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(wait_event_type, 'CPU') AS "Wait Type", COALESCE(wait_event, 'Running') AS "Wait Event", state AS "State", COUNT(*) AS "Count" FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE pid <> pg_backend_pid() GROUP BY wait_event_type, wait_event, state ORDER BY "Count" DESC LIMIT 30

panels.title: Wait Events Detail

panels.description: Count of connected streaming replication standbys.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM pg_stat_replication

panels.title: Replication Standby Count

panels.description: Full streaming replication status including LSN positions and total lag.

targets.rawSql: SELECT client_addr::text AS "Standby IP", usename AS "User", application_name AS "App", state AS "State", sync_state AS "Sync Mode", sent_lsn::text AS "Sent LSN", write_lsn::text AS "Write LSN", flush_lsn::text AS "Flush LSN", replay_lsn::text AS "Replay LSN", pg_size_pretty(pg_wal_lsn_diff(sent_lsn, replay_lsn)) AS "Total Lag" FROM pg_stat_replication ORDER BY pg_wal_lsn_diff(sent_lsn, replay_lsn) DESC

panels.title: Streaming Replication Status

panels.description: Replication slot WAL lag — high values can exhaust disk space on the primary.

targets.rawSql: SELECT slot_name AS "Slot", slot_type AS "Type", active AS "Active", pg_size_pretty(pg_wal_lsn_diff(pg_current_wal_lsn(), restart_lsn)) AS "Lag (readable)", pg_wal_lsn_diff(pg_current_wal_lsn(), restart_lsn) AS "Lag (bytes)" FROM pg_replication_slots ORDER BY pg_wal_lsn_diff(pg_current_wal_lsn(), restart_lsn) DESC

panels.title: Replication Slot WAL Lag

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema') AND table_type = 'BASE TABLE'
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM pg_indexes WHERE schemaname NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema')
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_schema NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema')
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM information_schema.routines WHERE routine_schema NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema')
SELECT pg_size_pretty(pg_database_size(current_database())) AS value
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM information_schema.schemata WHERE schema_name NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema','pg_toast')

panels.description: All databases: size, active connections, transaction counts, rollback rate.

targets.rawSql: SELECT datname AS "Database", pg_size_pretty(pg_database_size(datname)) AS "Size", pg_database_size(datname) AS "Size (bytes)", numbackends AS "Active Backends", xact_commit AS "Txn Commits", xact_rollback AS "Txn Rollbacks", ROUND(100.0 * xact_rollback / NULLIF(xact_commit + xact_rollback, 0), 2) AS "Rollback %", tup_inserted AS "Rows Inserted", tup_updated AS "Rows Updated", tup_deleted AS "Rows Deleted" FROM pg_stat_database WHERE datname NOT IN ('template0','template1') ORDER BY pg_database_size(datname) DESC

panels.title: Database Size & Transaction Overview

panels.description: Objects per schema: tables, indexes, views, materialized views, sequences.

targets.rawSql: SELECT n.nspname AS "Schema", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE c.relkind = 'r') AS "Tables", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE c.relkind = 'i') AS "Indexes", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE c.relkind = 'v') AS "Views", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE c.relkind = 'm') AS "Mat Views", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE c.relkind = 'S') AS "Sequences", pg_size_pretty(SUM(pg_relation_size(c.oid))) AS "Total Size" FROM pg_class c JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace WHERE n.nspname NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema','pg_toast') GROUP BY n.nspname ORDER BY "Tables" DESC

panels.title: Schema Object Summary

panels.description: Key performance and memory settings from pg_settings.

targets.rawSql: SELECT name AS "Setting", setting AS "Value", unit AS "Unit", short_desc AS "Description" FROM pg_settings WHERE name IN ('max_connections','shared_buffers','effective_cache_size','work_mem','maintenance_work_mem','wal_buffers','checkpoint_completion_target','random_page_cost','effective_io_concurrency','max_wal_size','min_wal_size','max_worker_processes','max_parallel_workers','autovacuum_max_workers','autovacuum_vacuum_cost_delay','autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor','autovacuum_analyze_scale_factor','log_min_duration_statement','idle_in_transaction_session_timeout','statement_timeout','lock_timeout') ORDER BY name

panels.title: Key PostgreSQL Settings

panels.description: Installed extensions in the current database.

targets.rawSql: SELECT name AS "Extension", default_version AS "Default Version", installed_version AS "Installed Version", comment AS "Description" FROM pg_available_extensions WHERE installed_version IS NOT NULL ORDER BY name

panels.title: Installed Extensions

panels.description: Roles and their permissions in this PostgreSQL instance.

targets.rawSql: SELECT rolname AS "Role", rolsuper AS "Superuser", rolinherit AS "Inherit", rolcreaterole AS "Create Role", rolcreatedb AS "Create DB", rolcanlogin AS "Can Login", rolreplication AS "Replication", rolbypassrls AS "Bypass RLS", rolconnlimit AS "Conn Limit", COALESCE(rolvaliduntil::text, 'Never') AS "Expires" FROM pg_roles ORDER BY rolname

panels.title: Roles & Permissions
📋 Grafana Enterprise Application Logs — samplef-dev  |  source: grafanacloud-acinonprodtru-logs

panels.description: Error rate per minute for samplef-grafana-enterprise in samplef-dev namespace. Spikes indicate alert evaluation storms, datasource failures, or DB connection pressure. Real incident data confirms bursts at 21:45–22:10 UTC on 2026-03-31.
panels.title: Log Volume by Level — samplef-dev

panels.description: Total error + warn log count over selected time range. Threshold: green < 50/min, orange 50–200/min, red > 200/min.
panels.title: Total Errors (range)

panels.description: Total warning count — includes authn failures, 404s on datasource resources, non-200 Loki responses.
panels.title: Total Warnings (range)

panels.description: Unique pods seen in samplef-dev. More than 1 = restarts / rollouts in progress.
panels.title: Active Pods (samplef-dev)

panels.description: Rate of ngalert.scheduler errors per minute — directly correlated with DB connection wait spikes. Breakdown by rule_uid shows which alert rules are failing. Confirmed error patterns: Snowflake TCP resets, loki-auth-test 404s, data source not found.
panels.title: Error Rate by Logger — samplef-dev

panels.description: ngalert.scheduler 'Failed to evaluate rule' errors broken down by error category. Key patterns from incident: Snowflake TCP reset, loki-auth-test 404, data source not found, remote write 401 (no org id), recording rule failure.

targets.expr: sum(rate({app="samplef-grafana-enterprise", namespace="samplef-dev"} | logfmt | logger="ngalert.scheduler" | msg="Failed to evaluate rule" | error=~".*snowflake.*" [$__interval]))
sum(rate({app="samplef-grafana-enterprise", namespace="samplef-dev"} | logfmt | logger="ngalert.scheduler" | msg="Failed to evaluate rule" | error=~".*data source not found.*" [$__interval]))
sum(rate({app="samplef-grafana-enterprise", namespace="samplef-dev"} | logfmt | logger="ngalert.scheduler" | msg="Failed to evaluate rule" | error=~".*remote write.*401.*" [$__interval]))
sum(rate({app="samplef-grafana-enterprise", namespace="samplef-dev"} | logfmt | logger="ngalert.scheduler" | msg="Failed to evaluate rule" | error=~".*404.*" [$__interval]))

panels.title: Alert Eval Failure Breakdown — samplef-dev

panels.description: tsdb.loki errors from samplef-dev pod (bjn2t) — loki-auth-test returning 404 for sum(rate({job="logsservice"}[5m])) queries. Sustained 404 errors across rule_uids cfgyxluz6ilmoa, efgzl6qcx3f28f, ffgyyzf6yj668a indicate the logsservice stream is empty or the datasource is misconfigured.

targets.expr: sum by (dsName, statusCode) (rate({app="samplef-grafana-enterprise", namespace="samplef-dev"} | logfmt | logger="tsdb.loki" | level="error" [$__interval]))

panels.title: Loki Datasource Errors by Source — samplef-dev

panels.description: authn.service 'Failed to authenticate request' — user token not found. These are /api/live/ws WebSocket upgrade failures (HTTP 401). Sustained burst means expired sessions trying to reconnect, likely triggered by the pod restart at ~21:36 UTC.

targets.expr: sum by (pod) (rate({app="samplef-grafana-enterprise", namespace="samplef-dev"} | logfmt | logger="authn.service" | error="user token not found" [$__interval]))

panels.title: Auth Failures & WebSocket 401s — samplef-dev

panels.description: Recording rule failures in samplef-dev: 'Recording rule evaluation failed after all attempts'. Rule afesk9m37fym8f fails with remote write HTTP 401 (no org id — Prometheus remote write misconfiguration). Rule ffgyyzf6yj668a fails silently with empty query result.

targets.expr: sum by (rule_uid) (rate({app="samplef-grafana-enterprise", namespace="samplef-dev"} | logfmt | logger="ngalert.scheduler" | msg=~"Recording rule evaluation failed.*" [$__interval]))
sum(rate({app="samplef-grafana-enterprise", namespace="samplef-dev"} | logfmt | logger="ngalert.scheduler" | msg="Failed to build rule evaluator" [$__interval]))

panels.title: Recording Rule & Evaluator Build Failures — samplef-dev

panels.description: recorded_queries.metric.writer errors — 'failed to get metrics: no results from query'. Rule 'test' returns empty result set. This is a non-critical but persistent noise error in samplef-dev.
panels.title: Recorded Query Writer Errors

panels.description: Alertmanager notification errors — 'Notify for alerts failed'. Key error: gomail invalid address missing '@' for rule in org 2. This is a broken alert contact point configuration.
panels.title: Alertmanager Notify Failures

panels.description: Loki alert rule query errors (tsdb.loki, log queries used as instant queries returning HTTP 400). Rule ae9hndmw0ham8a: 'log queries are not supported as an instant query type' — must be changed to range queryType.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({app="samplef-grafana-enterprise", namespace="samplef-dev"} | logfmt | logger="tsdb.loki" | error=~".*log queries are not supported as an instant query.*" [$__range]))

panels.title: Log-as-Instant Query Errors (HTTP 400)

panels.description: Live error log stream for samplef-grafana-enterprise in samplef-dev. Structured fields parsed via logfmt. Use the time range picker to zoom into the 21:45–22:10 UTC incident window.
panels.title: Live Error Log Stream — samplef-dev

panels.description: Top recurring error fingerprints — identifies which unique error patterns are most frequent. Use this to prioritize which alert rules or datasources to fix first.

targets.expr: sum by (logger, msg) (count_over_time({app="samplef-grafana-enterprise", namespace="samplef-dev"} | logfmt | level="error" [$__range]))

panels.title: Top Error Patterns by Logger + Message — samplef-dev

title: Advanced PostgreSQL Monitoring — SAMPLEF Dev
